title: Jump Game
description:
## Jump Game

You are given an integer array `nums` where `nums[i]` represents the maximum number of steps you can jump forward from position `i`. Starting at index 0, determine if you can reach the last index.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= nums.length <= 10^4`
- `0 <= nums[i] <= 10^5`

examples.explanation: Jump 1 from index 0 to 1, then 3 from index 1 to reach index 4.
You always reach index 3 with 0 jumps remaining and can't proceed.

hiddenCases.explanation: Can only reach index 1, which has 0
Three consecutive zeros at indices 35-37 block the path

solutions.label: Greedy — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Track `max_reach` — the farthest index reachable so far. At each position `i`, if `i > max_reach` we're stuck and return False. Otherwise, update `max_reach` with `i + nums[i]`. If we finish the loop, we can reach the end. The key insight: if we can reach index `i`, we can reach any index up to `i + nums[i]`.

solutions.label: DP — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Mark each index as reachable or not. Index 0 is always reachable. For each index i, check all previous indices j: if j is reachable AND j + nums[j] >= i, then i is reachable. O(n²) because we check all previous indices for each position. The greedy approach reduces this to O(n) by tracking only the maximum reach.

hints: Track the maximum index you can reach from positions visited so far.
If at any point your current index exceeds the max reachable index, you're stuck and return False.
